meta.ministry: Government of Maharashtra, General Administration Department
meta.project_subject: Third-Party Evaluation of the Initiative for Increasing Efficiency in Decision Making
meta.deliverables: Develop questionnaire & data collection framework; consult 2 departments; circulate questionnaires to 32 Mantralaya Departments and 36 Districts; analyse submitted documents and responses; prepare and submit two final reports—one department-wise and one district-wise—highlighting overall findings, comparative analysis, best practices and areas of improvement.

content.full_text: The General Administration Department of the Government of Maharashtra has approved Quality Council of India (QCI) on nomination basis to conduct a Third-Party Evaluation of the state’s Initiative for Increasing Efficiency in Decision Making. QCI will develop a questionnaire and data framework based on its earlier exercise for DARPG, consult two departments, and then collect data from all 32 Mantralaya Departments and 36 Districts. Three QCI resources will be stationed with the state for framework finalisation and six additional resources (three teams) will carry out evaluation. Activities span questionnaire finalisation (5 person-days, 3 professionals), questionnaire filling (15 person-days, 8 professionals), analysis & evaluation (10 person-days, 6 professionals) and report submission (10 person-days, 3 professionals) over eight weeks. The total approved cost is Rs 18,90,000 including 12% administrative cost; travel/lodging in Maharashtra and applicable GST will be charged extra. Final deliverables are two reports—department-wise and district-wise—covering findings, comparative analysis, best practices and improvement areas.
